summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document via the generic yaml-frontmatter adapter, checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Two categories of kind — ownership, not mechanism

gate.reason: the curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md are ratification territory (external-fact citations to the Claude Code subagent docs, outside build's fence) and must be human-authored first; the in-fence binding + tests go green only once they are on disk. On un-park plan re-scopes files to the in-fence engine slice.

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under provider claude-code beside kinds/claude-code/{skill,rule,memory} with `provider = "claude-code"`: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, `format = "yaml-frontmatter"`, composed extraction over `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register `("claude-code.agent", AGENT_PACKAGE)` in QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.
Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list (kind.rs:30) so the emit/drift lock path (src/drift.rs) recognizes an agent as a projected built-in layer, not a custom kind.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Revived 07-04 (John, inbox): revival condition met — whole-harness governance + first non-dogfood adoption. Mostly curated data, near-zero engine (a binding + tests). Curated KIND.md/PACKAGE.md are human-authored (outside build's fence); on un-park plan re-scopes files to the in-fence slice only. Shares src/builtin.rs + src/kind.rs with COMMAND-KIND — serialize the two when both un-park.

summary: Add the `command` built-in harness kind — `.claude/commands/*.md` slash-command members via the generic yaml-frontmatter adapter, checked by an embedded `command.anthropic` package

per.section: Two categories of kind — ownership, not mechanism

gate.reason: the curated kinds/claude-code/command/KIND.md + packages/command.anthropic/PACKAGE.md are ratification territory (external-fact citations to the Claude Code slash-command docs, outside build's fence) and must be human-authored first; the in-fence binding + tests go green only once they are on disk.

new.description: The embedded built-in `command` KIND.md (curated, outside build's fence), `provider = "claude-code"`: `governs = { root = '.claude/commands', glob = '*.md' }`, `format = "yaml-frontmatter"`, composed extraction over the documented slash-command frontmatter fields (`description`, optional `argument-hint`/`model`/`allowed-tools`) plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it.

The command kind's built-in package `command.anthropic` (curated, outside build's fence). Clauses over the documented Claude Code slash-command surface, each with a per-clause `source` citation to the custom-commands docs; an advisory `max_lines`.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken command per clause proving it fires, a clean command that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add the `COMMAND_PACKAGE = "command.anthropic"` const and register `("claude-code.command", COMMAND_PACKAGE)` in QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md.
Add `"command"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list (kind.rs:30) so the emit/drift lock path recognizes a command as a projected built-in layer.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the command package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean command; import of an unchanged command is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/commands/*.md` to the `command.anthropic` package; governing `.claude/commands` auto-retires its `coverage.unmodeled-surface` finding (coverage_note::governed_by_any — no coverage_note.rs edit); import projects command members idempotently; gates green.
notes: Filed 07-04 (John, inbox). Markdown/yaml-frontmatter kind like agent — adapter exists, engine is a binding + tests. Curated files human-authored (fence-excluded); parked on that. Serialize after AGENT-KIND (shares src/builtin.rs QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS + src/kind.rs BUILTIN_KINDS). Advisory-retirement is latent — temper's own harness carries no .claude/commands surface today.

summary: Add the `settings` built-in harness kind — `.claude/settings.json` as a JSON-manifest member with nested key-path fields, checked by an embedded `settings.anthropic` package

per.section: Two categories of kind — ownership, not mechanism

gate.reason: rests on the (json-projection-format) fork — reading settings.json needs a `json` projection format harvested into 15-kinds.md's closed vocabulary + a generic JSON adapter, neither yet in the corpus — plus human-authored curated KIND.md/PACKAGE.md (outside build's fence). Un-parks when the fork resolves and the curated files land.

new.description: The embedded built-in `settings` KIND.md (curated, outside build's fence), `provider = "claude-code"`: `governs = { root = '.claude', glob = 'settings.json' }`, `format = "json"` (the fork's new vocabulary entry), composed extraction over nested key-path `field` primitives (permissions, env, hooks) consuming the now-shipped key-path `Primitive::Field` (`extract::resolve_key_path`, src/kind.rs:839). build.rs auto-embeds it.

The settings kind's built-in package `settings.anthropic` (curated, outside build's fence). Decidable clauses over documented settings.json keys, each with a per-clause `source` citation to code.claude.com/docs/en/settings.

Fixture-driven proof: one broken settings.json per clause proving it fires, a clean one that trips nothing, and an import round-trip snapshot — through the generic JSON adapter, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add the `SETTINGS_PACKAGE = "settings.anthropic"` const and register `("claude-code.settings", SETTINGS_PACKAGE)` in QUALIFIED_FLOOR_BINDINGS. The `json` Format variant + adapter belong to the fork's own shared engine entry, not here — this entry rests on them.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the settings package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean settings.json; nested key-path fields read the intended values.

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/settings.json` to `settings.anthropic` reading nested key-path fields; governing settings.json retires the `coverage.unmodeled-surface` advisory (the live marker on temper's own harness); gates green. (Held: not built until the json fork resolves + curated files land.)
notes: Filed 07-04 (John, inbox): retirer of the live settings.json coverage.unmodeled-surface advisory. JSON-manifest → dependsOnForks (json-projection-format); the shared json adapter is that fork's engine entry. EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS' key-path Field has SHIPPED (1b9f33a) — ready; only the json format harvest + adapter remain. MCP + plugin/marketplace kinds ride the same fork (held there, not filed).

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (the authoring-face manifest).

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB root `package.json` is the *private* flume manifest (≠ sdk/package.json) — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
